title: hieracium guthnikianum subsp. erythrodes
intro: Hieracium guthnikianum subsp. erythrodes is a rare, lesser-known subspecies of the hawkweed plant, characterized by its distinctive red or reddish-brown flower heads. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to be derived from its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Hieracium guthnikianum subsp. erythrodes include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a dried or fresh herb, and may be infused in teas, tinctures, or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
